title: packera clevelandii var. clevelandii
intro: Packera clevelandii var. clevelandii, also known as Cleveland's ragwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its rich content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of Packera clevelandii var. clevelandii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to leverage its medicinal properties.
